title: dry earthy german lamb casserole
description: earthy and crumbly german lamb casserole made with ras el hanout and a mother sauce base, perfect for a solo dinner
instructions: season lamb cubes with salt and pepper, then dust with flour and shake off excess.
heat oil in a large frying pan over medium heat, brown lamb cubes in batches until browned all over.
remove lamb from pan and set aside, then add chopped onion, carrot and celery and cook until softened, about 8 minutes.
add minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
add ras el hanout and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
add beef broth, red wine (if using) and tomato paste, stir to combine.
bring mixture to the boil, then transfer to a 20x30cm baking dish.
arrange browned lamb cubes on top of the sauce, leaving a 2cm gap between each cube.
cover with aluminium foil and bake for 1 hour, then remove foil and bake for a further 30 minutes.
remove from oven and let rest for 10 minutes before serving.
slice into 4 portions and serve hot.
